\`A. Pulchra Healing General Observations by Day

Characteristics were taken quantitatively in gene expression study with yes/no:

| hole in center | polyp in hole | wound smoothed | pigment over wound | tip exist | tip extension | New corallites on tip |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |

Averaged proportion of character traits by temperature treatment:

![][image1]

General Observation and Trends across unheated corals:

| Day | Physical Characteristic |
| :---- | :---- |
| 0 | Blunt wound; coarse skeleton, no tissue |
| 1 | Same as D0 |
| 2 | Skeletal smoothing begins, possible axial polyp hole begins defined |
| 3 | Skeletal smoothing continues, axial polyp hole becomes more defined |
| 4 | Likely an obvious axial polyp hole present with axial polyp present, very likely rounded out skeleton  |
| 5 |  |
| 6 | Definitive axial hole with polyp and smoothed over skeleton, 50% chance of tip/axial corallite beginning formation |
| 7 |  |
| 8 | Greater chance of axial corallite existing, and possible extension |
| 9 |  |
| 10 | Corallite extension continues |
| 11 |  |
| 12 | New corallites begin forming on axial corallite like branches on a tree |
| 13 | New corallites continue forming on axial corallite, polyps may be present in these corallites |
| 14 |  |
| 15 |  |
| 16 | \*\*\*Possibly next step is pigmentation of axial polyps |
| 17 |  |
| 18 |  |
| 19 |  |
| 20 |  |

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
